intent: Build a simple Python task management library (tasklib). It is not a production system. Its purpose is to validate that the Crafted Dev Agent build pipeline can close a complete dependency chain — from documentation through scaffold to working code — with real merges at each step. Resolve any open issues using the PRD process and best practices.
scope_statement:
Build a minimal Python task management library (tasklib) as specified in TRD-TASKLIB, comprising a documentation set (README, ARCHITECTURE, API reference), a Python package scaffold under src/tasklib/ with models/ and storage/ subpackages, a Task dataclass with TaskStatus enumeration (TRD-TASKLIB §3.1, FR-1 through FR-7), an InMemoryTaskStore (TRD-TASKLIB §3.2, FR-8 through FR-14), a CLI entry point (TRD-TASKLIB §3.3, FR-15 through FR-19), and a package root re-exporting the public API (TRD-TASKLIB §3.4, FR-20). The build validates the Crafted Dev Agent pipeline's ability to close a full dependency chain — docs → scaffold → model → storage → CLI — with real merges at each step (TRD-TASKLIB §1).

prd_plan.title: Documentation Set — README, ARCHITECTURE, and API Reference
Package Scaffold — Directory Tree, Placeholder Files, and Import-Safe Boundaries
Task Model — TaskStatus Enumeration and Task Dataclass with Serialization
CLI Entry Point — Add, List, and Complete Commands
Package Root Re-exports, API Documentation Amendment, and End-to-End Integration Tests

1.title: Add tasklib documentation set — README, ARCHITECTURE, API reference
1.summary: Creates the foundational documentation set under docs/tasklib/ as specified in PRD-001. README.md describes purpose, package structure, stdlib-only installation, and working usage examples for Task creation, InMemoryTaskStore usage, and CLI invocation. ARCHITECTURE.md explains the layered dependency design (docs → scaffold → model → storage → CLI) with an ASCII dependency diagram. API.md documents Task dataclass fields, TaskStatus enumeration values, all InMemoryTaskStore method signatures with parameter/return types, and CLI commands with usage examples. This is the dependency-chain root — no code, no tests, docs only.

2.title: Add tasklib Python package scaffold
2.summary: Creates the directory skeleton for the tasklib package under src/tasklib/ with models/, storage/, and cli/ subpackages. Each directory gets an __init__.py file with minimal docstrings only. No logic, no dataclasses, no imports of business types — purely structural. Validates that the scaffold merges cleanly after docs.

3.title: Implement Task dataclass and TaskStatus enumeration
3.summary: Implements the Task dataclass and TaskStatus enum in src/tasklib/models/task.py. TaskStatus has TODO, IN_PROGRESS, and DONE values. Task has fields: id (UUID4 string, auto-generated), title (str), description (str, default empty), status (TaskStatus, default TODO), created_at (datetime, auto-set), and updated_at (datetime, auto-set). Uses only stdlib (uuid, datetime, dataclasses, enum). Updates models/__init__.py to re-export Task and TaskStatus.

4.title: Implement InMemoryTaskStore
4.summary: Implements InMemoryTaskStore in src/tasklib/storage/memory.py. Provides add(task), get(task_id), list_all(), update(task_id, **fields), and delete(task_id) methods. Uses a dict keyed by task ID. Raises KeyError for missing tasks on get/update/delete. update() sets updated_at automatically. Updates storage/__init__.py to re-export InMemoryTaskStore.

5.title: Implement CLI entry point for tasklib
5.summary: Implements the CLI module in src/tasklib/cli/main.py using argparse (stdlib) with subcommands: add (title, optional description), list (prints all tasks), update (task_id, status), and delete (task_id). Each command instantiates an InMemoryTaskStore, performs the operation, and prints results to stdout. Includes a __main__.py for `python -m tasklib` invocation.

6.title: Add package root public API re-exports
6.summary: Updates src/tasklib/__init__.py to re-export the public API surface: Task, TaskStatus, and InMemoryTaskStore. Defines __all__ listing all public symbols. This is the final implementation PR that closes the dependency chain and completes the documented import surface.

7.title: Add integration tests for full tasklib dependency chain
7.summary: End-to-end integration tests validating the complete tasklib pipeline: create tasks via the model, store them via InMemoryTaskStore, retrieve/update/delete, and exercise the CLI entry point via subprocess. Verifies that the public API re-exports work correctly and that the full dependency chain (docs → scaffold → model → storage → CLI → re-exports) is coherent. Uses only unittest from stdlib.

8.title: Update tasklib API reference for finalized interfaces
8.summary: Amends docs/tasklib/API.md to reflect the finalized implemented signatures, exact parameter names, return types, exception behavior, public imports from the package root, and CLI usage details after all code has landed. This is a dedicated update PR — not a re-creation.
